MINI MINUTES:
Appearances: Emilie Calhoun appearing by Zoom for Byron Wilson who also appeared by Zoom. Sheila Robello appearing by Zoom for David Keeler who also appeared by Zoom. Debi Ramos appearing by CourtCall for Frances Petrocelli.

The Court and counsel confer regarding the term sheet, remainder beneficiaries, etc.
Argument/discussions are held. The Court inquired if the parties were willing to meet with Judge Jeffrey Ross in Department 502 for a settlement conference. Additional argument and discussions are held.

At the conclusion of argument/discussions, the Court DENIED the Petition for Instructions without prejudice, referred the parties to Judge Jeffrey Ross in Department 502 for a settlement conference, and provided the following contact information for Judge Ross: JRoss@sftc.org. The Court stated that the Trustee needs to file a petition, that it would set this matter for a status in thirty days, and continued the matters on calendar today to October 23, 2020 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. Ms. Robello stated that she would contact Judge Ross and inquired if the matter could be specially set if a petition was to be filed. The Court stated that the petition could be specially set and directed Ms. Robello to contact Probate Examiner Sean Bang to obtain a date. The clerk requested clarification from the Court. The Court confirmed that the Petition for Instructions was DENIED without prejudice.

The remaining petitions on calendar are continued to October 23, 2020 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: John K. Stewart. Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda. Court Reporter (by Zoom): Peggy Tsujimoto, CSR #5229, Tel. 415.828.1919, Email: p2gmoto@earthlink.net.

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Dismiss And For Sanctions
Off Calendar Mar-07-2019 Denied
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Emilie Calhoun and Elena Protich for and with Bryon Wilson (Peitioner). Eric George and Peter Obstler for and with Frances Petrocelli (Respondent).

Mr. George presented his oral argument in support of his objections to the Court's tentative ruling on the motion to dismiss and for sanctions.

After hearing oral argument and reading the court filed documents the Court adopted the tentative ruling as follows:

The Motion to Dismiss and for Sanctions under CCP 128.5 and 128.7 is denied. The moving party has not shown that the petitioner's conduct in bringing the pending Petition is "objectively unreasonable," i.e. "totally and completely without merit." (Peake v. Underwood (2014) 227 Cal.App.4th 428, 441, quoting In re marriage of Flaherty (1982) 31 Cal.3d 637, 650). In fact, Petitioner did demonstrate a reasonable basis for bringing the Petition, see for example: Exhibits A and B to Declaration of Rebecca Cohn in Opposition to the Motion; Exhibits B, C, and E to Declaration of Byron Wilson in Opposition to the Motion; and Exhibits K, L, M, O, N, U, and V to Declaration of Elina Protich in Opposition to the Motion. It is not improper for a petitioner to plead facts on information and belief, so long as the petitioner also alleges facts on which the belief is founded, and believes in good faith that the allegations are true. Here, Petitioner makes allegations on information and belief, and states the facts upon which his belief is based. There is no showing that Petitioner is bringing his claims in bad faith, or solely to harass Respondent. Although Respondent has produced declarations by decedent's doctors and attorney refuting the allegation that decedent lacked testamentary capacity or suffered from dementia when the Trust was amended, it appears that those declarations and other discovery referenced in the Motion were produced after the First Amended Petition was filed, and that Respondent filed this Motion to Dismiss after Petitioner requested a stipulated Second Amended Petition after discovery of new facts.
Prevailing party to submit a form of order approved as to form.
